response:
Alex has always had a strong belief in traditional values, especially when it comes to love and relationships. This month, they decided to challenge themselves by reading three different novels. Each book offered a unique perspective on love that was quite different from what Alex had previously encountered. The first novel was a short but impactful read, containing exactly 120 pages. It took Alex four days to finish, which meant they read a consistent number of pages each day. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages, and it took Alex six days to complete. This made Alex realize that the time spent reading varied with the number of pages. The third novel was the longest, with 240 pages, and it took Alex eight days to finish. This pattern showed that the number of days increased proportionally with the number of pages. Alex was curious about how many pages they read each day on average. To find the total pages read per day, they needed to calculate the average across all three novels. 

For the first novel, 120 pages over four days means Alex read 30 pages per day. The second novel, with 180 pages over six days, also resulted in 30 pages per day. The third novel, with 240 pages over eight days, again gave 30 pages per day. This consistency in reading speed was surprising and impressive. Alex was able to maintain the same pace throughout all three books. This uniformity helped Alex stay focused and engaged with the material. Each novel had a different theme, but the reading pace remained the same. The first book explored love in a more traditional setting, while the second introduced a more modern viewpoint. The third novel took the discussion even further, presenting a completely new perspective. 

Reading the same number of pages each day helped Alex manage their time effectively. It also made the reading process feel more structured and manageable. The first novel’s brevity made it easier to finish quickly, while the second required more time and attention. The third novel was the most demanding, both in length and in content. Despite the differences in the books, the reading pace stayed constant. This consistency allowed Alex to compare the themes and messages of each novel more easily. The first book’s message was clear and direct, while the second was more complex and layered. The third novel challenged Alex’s understanding of love in a profound way. 

Alex found it interesting that the same reading pace could be applied to books of different lengths. This made the reading experience
